Home › Books › Dark Life Series

Middle Grade · Ages 9-12 · Grades 5-7

Dark Life Series

A subsea Western set on the flooded frontier of tomorrow. The last frontier is not West. It is Down.

About the Series

The World of Dark Life

In a future Earth half-drowned by rising seas, humanity has been pushed to overcrowded inland cities except for a brave few who have claimed the ocean floor as the last frontier. The subsea settlers are ranchers, farmers, and pioneers. They are also hiding a secret.

Children born and raised underwater develop abilities that Topsiders would fear, hunt, and exploit. Fifteen-year-old Ty is one of them. When Topsider orphan Gemma arrives searching for her missing brother, she and Ty uncover a conspiracy that could destroy everything the settlers have built and expose what the ocean has made them.

Part frontier Western, part sci-fi thriller, with real deep-sea science woven into every page.

The Real Science Inside

Dark Life is built on real ocean science: deep-sea ecosystems, bioluminescence, underwater pressure physiology, and NOAA research into subsea habitation. Great for STEM/STEAM connections in grades 5-7.

Educator Resources & STEM Guides →

    Dark Life

    May 1, 2010
  2. 2

    Rip Tide

    August 1, 2011

  • Disney Film Option
  • 10-state award nominations

The Books

Dark Life

May 1, 2010 · 9–12 · Grades 5–7

In a future where rising seas have flooded the coasts, 15-year-old Ty has grown up farming the ocean floor — the only boy born and raised in the subsea settlements. When Topsider orphan Gemma arrives searching for her brother, the two uncover a dangerous secret about the Seablite Gang and the mysterious "dark gifts" that underwater life gives to its children.

Read More →

Rip Tide

August 1, 2011 · 9–12 · Grades 5–7

Ty and Gemma return to a subsea territory under siege. When settlers are found chained to a sunken submarine, Ty must confront new threats — and harder truths about his dark gift — before the ocean floor becomes uninhabitable for everyone.

Read More →
Kat Falls

NYT bestselling author of Dark Life and Inhuman.
Evanston, IL.
